---
title: "CIND 820 -Project"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-08"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r libraries}
#install.packages("cansim")
#install.packages("dplyr")
#install.packages("tidyr")
#install.packages("tidyverse")
#install.packages("corrplot")
library(cansim)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(corrplot)
```



```{r Load Tables}
df_age<- get_cansim("35-10-006-01")
df_geo<- get_cansim("35-10-0156-01")
df_total<-get_cansim("35-10-0068-01")
df_indID<- get_cansim("35-10-0125-01")
df_ID_UrbRural<-get_cansim("35-10-0127-01")
```

```{r cleaning}
#remove columns and remove date rows outside bounds

df_age_clean<- df_age[-c(2,4,5,11:30)]

df_age_clean <- df_age_clean[df_age_clean$REF_DATE>="2014"&df_age_clean$REF_DATE<='2024',]


df_geo_clean <- df_geo[-c(2,4,5,10:27)]

df_indID_clean <- df_indID[-c(2,4,5,10:27)]

df_total_clean <- df_total[-c(2,4,5,8:21)]

##select rows with conditions
df_total_clean <- df_total_clean[df_total_clean$REF_DATE>="2014"&df_total_clean$REF_DATE<="2024",]

df_ID_UrbRural <- df_ID_UrbRural[-c(2,4,5,10:27)]



##spread value types

df_age_clean <- df_age_clean %>% pivot_wider(names_from = `Victims and accused`, values_from = VALUE)
df_geo_clean<- df_geo_clean %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Homicides, values_from = VALUE)
df_indID_clean<- df_indID_clean %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Statistics, values_from = VALUE)
df_total_clean<- df_total_clean %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Homicides, values_from = VALUE)
df_ID_UrbRural<- df_ID_UrbRural %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Statistics, values_from = VALUE)


#remove numbers not of interest
df_age_clean <-df_age_clean[-c(7)]

df_total_clean <- df_total_clean[-c(5,6)]

#Removing abnormal data
df_age_clean <- df_age_clean[df_age_clean$Gender!= "Unidentifiable human remains",]
df_age_clean <- df_age_clean[df_age_clean$`Age group`!= "Age unknown",]

df_geo_clean <- df_geo_clean[df_geo_clean$Gender!= "Unidentifiable human remains",]

#adding log scale for age dataframe
df_age_clean$"Log Number of Homicides" <- log(df_age_clean$`Number of homicide victims`)


```

```{r Data Exploration}
#summary stats
summary(df_age_clean)
unique(df_age_clean$`Indigenous identity`)
summary(df_geo_clean)
summary(df_indID_clean)
unique(df_indID_clean$`Indigenous Identity`)

#fairly sure the total table is not needed
summary(df_total_clean)



##Summary tables
summary_ID_indID <- df_indID_clean %>% group_by(`Indigenous Identity`,Gender,REF_DATE) %>%
  summarise(average_num_hom= mean(`Number of homicide victims`, na.rm=TRUE), 
            average_rate_hom= mean(`Homicide rates per 100,000 population`, na.rm=TRUE),
            max_num_hom= max(`Number of homicide victims`), max_rate_hom= max(`Homicide rates per 100,000 population`),
            .groups = 'drop')

summary_ID_age <- df_age_clean %>% group_by(`Indigenous identity`, `Age group`, Gender, REF_DATE) %>%
  summarise(average_num_hom= mean(`Number of homicide victims`, na.rm=TRUE),
            max_num_hom= max(`Number of homicide victims`),
            .groups = 'drop')

summary_ID_geo <- df_geo_clean %>% group_by(`Indigenous identity`, Gender, REF_DATE, GEO) %>%
  summarise(average_num_hom= mean(`Number of homicide victims`, na.rm=TRUE),
            max_num_hom= max(`Number of homicide victims`),
            .groups = 'drop')

summary_ID_total <- df_total_clean %>% group_by(REF_DATE, GEO) %>%
  summarise(average_num_hom= mean(`Number of homicide victims`, na.rm=TRUE),
            max_num_hom= max(`Number of homicide victims`),
            .groups = 'drop')



# Check columns with quantitative data for missing values

sum((is.na(df_age_clean$`Number of homicide victims`)))
sum((is.na(df_age_clean$`Age group`)))

sum((is.na(df_geo_clean$`Number of homicide victims`)))
sum((is.na(df_geo_clean$`Homicide rates per 100,000 population`)))

sum((is.na(df_indID_clean$`Number of homicide victims`)))
sum((is.na(df_indID_clean$`Homicide rates per 100,000 population`)))

sum((is.na(df_total_clean$`Number of homicide victims`)))
sum((is.na(df_total_clean$`Homicide rates per 100,000 population`)))


```

```{r Histograms and Boxplots}

##note arge skew added natural log to value scale
hist(df_age_clean$`Log Number of Homicides`)
hist(df_age_clean$`Number of homicide victims`)

hist(log(df_geo_clean$`Number of homicide victims`))
#normally distributed with log
hist(log(df_geo_clean$`Homicide rates per 100,000 population`))

hist(log(df_indID_clean$`Number of homicide victims`))
hist(log(df_indID_clean$`Homicide rates per 100,000 population`))

hist(log(df_total_clean$`Number of homicide victims`))
hist(log(df_total_clean$`Homicide rates per 100,000 population`))

##rates per 100000 are more consistent when log applied

#explore boxplots
boxplot(log(df_geo_clean$`Homicide rates per 100,000 population`)~df_geo_clean$GEO, xlab = df_geo_clean$GEO)


```


```{r Joining Tables}
#choose to join tables with the most obs to least obs
data_total1<- full_join(df_geo_clean,df_age_clean, by =c("REF_DATE", "Indigenous identity","Gender", "Number of homicide victims"))
#remove rows that do not provide new or accurate detail
data_total1.2<- data_total1[-c(6,7,8,10)]


##Ad a new column that is factor matched to the indID column

df_indID_clean <- df_indID_clean %>%
                    mutate(ID = case_when(
                    `Indigenous Identity`=="Indigenous identity" ~ "Indigenous identity",
                    `Indigenous Identity`=="Non-Indigenous identity" ~"Non-Indigenous identity",
                    `Indigenous Identity`=="Unknown Indigenous identity"~"Unknown Indigenous identity",
                    `Indigenous Identity`=="Total, by Indigenous identity" ~ "Total, by Indigenous identity",
                    `Indigenous Identity`=="First Nations (North American Indian)" ~ "Indigenous identity",
                    `Indigenous Identity`=="Inuk (Inuit)" ~"Indigenous identity",
                    `Indigenous Identity`=="Métis"~"Indigenous identity",
                    `Indigenous Identity`=="Indigenous person, Indigenous group unknown"~"Indigenous identity"))

df_indID_clean<- df_indID_clean%>%
                  rename(ID=`Indigenous Identity`, "Indigenous identity"=ID)


data_total2.2<- full_join(df_indID_clean,df_age_clean, by=c("REF_DATE","Gender", "Indigenous identity", "GEO","Number of homicide victims"))

#created a column that should have the same values for ind identity while preserving the meaning of the detailed column

data_total2<- full_join(data_total1.2,data_total2.2, by=c("REF_DATE","Gender", "Indigenous identity", "Number of homicide victims", "Age group"), relationship="many-to-many")

data_total3<- data_total2[-c(7,9,10)]

##check out table
summary(data_total3$`Number of homicide victims`)
hist(log(data_total3$`Number of homicide victims`))

##reorder final table
data_table<- data_total3 %>%
              relocate(`Number of homicide victims`, .after = "ID")

#cleaning new table
data_table_clean2 <- data_table[data_table$`Indigenous identity` != "Total, homicide victims by Indigenous identity",]

data_table_clean<- data_table %>% drop_na()
sum(duplicated(data_table_clean))                    

```

```{r Check Distribution}
#apply log
data_table_clean$"Log Num Homicides" <- log(data_table_clean$`Number of homicide victims`)
hist(data_table_clean$`Log Num Homicides`)
hist(data_table_clean$`Number of homicide victims`)
```


```{r Correlations}
#run correlation test on variables in combined table

corr_matrix<- cor(data_table_clean2, na.rm=TRUE, method = "pearson")

```
